mnemonics: My Very Educated Mother Just Served Us Nine Pizzas (Mnemonic for Planet order, adapted for Block Header: Merkle Root, Version, (Bits/E)Difficulty, (Previous Hash/M)Hash, (Timestamp/S)Time, Nonce)
keyPoints: The block header is an 80-byte section containing the metadata for the block.
It includes six key components: Version, Previous Block Hash, Merkle Root, Timestamp, Difficulty Target (Bits), and Nonce.
The header is what is hashed by miners to create the Proof-of-Work, linking each block cryptographically to the one before it.
commonMistakes: Confusing the Merkle Root (a hash of all transactions) with the Previous Block Hash (the hash of the prior block's header).
Thinking the Nonce is the only variable miners change; they can also change the Timestamp and the Merkle Root (via the coinbase transaction).
